title: Mag 7 earnings bonanza and Powell's home stretch: What to watch this week
body: Oops, something went wrong Coming off a strong Friday and fresh record highs for the stock market (and Nvidia ), investors look forward to the busiest earnings slate of the season. The S&P 500 ( ^GSPC ) closed Friday up 0.8% for a 0.6% gain for the week, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq ( ^IXIC ) finished Friday up 1.6% for a 1.5% return over the five-day stretch. The Dow Jones Industrial Average ( ^DJI ) fell 0.2% on Friday for a 0.4% loss for the week. The Iran situation may remain uncomfortably fluid for many, but investors will have plenty of other catalysts to answer the market's questions — and raise new ones. Calendar highlights The busiest earnings week of the quarter and Jerome Powell's second-to-last meeting as chair of the Federal Reserve greet investors heading into a jam-packed

title: The Federal Reserve's April Inflation Forecast Has Been Updated, and It's a Good News-Bad News Scenario for Wall Street
body: Last week was another history-maker for Wall Street, with the benchmark S&P 500 ( ^GSPC +0.80% ) and iconic Nasdaq Composite ( ^IXIC +1.63% ) launching to record-closing highs. Although the Dow Jones Industrial Average ( ^DJI 0.16% ) didn't reach a record high, it's a stone's throw away from one. Based on the performance of these indexes, you'd assume the U.S. economy and Wall Street are firing on all cylinders. But according to the latest inflation update from the Federal Reserve, things may not be as rosy as the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite imply . Fed Chair Jerome Powell delivering remarks. title: Stock Futures Slide With Markets on Edge Ahead of Big Tech Earnings
body: LIVE Last Updated: Updated 26 min ago By George Glover Stocks were wavering on Monday, with investors seemingly reluctant to make any major moves ahead of a slew of Big Tech earnings and a Federal Reserve interest-rate decision. Futures tracking the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 45 points, or 0.1%. S&P 500 futures slipped 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 were up 0.1%. Both the S&P and the Nasdaq hit record closing highs at the end of last week, powered higher by Intel and other chip stocks . Megacap tech earnings will decide if the rally lasts, with Google parent Alphabet, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, and Facebook owner Meta Platforms all set to report this week.

title: Intel’s Big Ride — Heard on the Street Weekly Recap
Dow Jones Futures: Tech Stocks, Oil Prices Rise Amid Iran News; Apple, Amazon, Google Lead Earnings
body: BREAKING: Tech Futures Rise; Big Earnings, Iran In Focus Investors.com will undergo scheduled maintenance on Sunday, April 26th. During this time, some features may be temporarily unavailable. Please update the IBD mobile app after Monday at 6:00 AM PT / 9:00 AM ET to access the latest feature improvements. Dow Jones futures edged lower early Monday, while S&P 500 futures and Nasdaq futures turned higher. U.S.-Iran talks were scrapped over the weekend but Tehran reportedly has made an offer to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Apple (AAPL), Amazon.com (AMZN), Google-parent Alphabet (GOOGL) headline an earnings wave.
